title: NUMERICAL METHODS IN ENGINEERING ANALYSIS
what: Five MATLAB implementations of classical numerical methods across real engineering domains: turbine power plant analysis (Newton-Raphson), thermal cost optimization (gradient descent), compressible duct flow (RK-4 ODE), three-mass vibration isolation (Forward Euler), and DC motor speed regulation (PID control)

Each problem reduced to a precise numerical formulation requiring convergence under engineering tolerances — turbine cold-end system solved to 10⁻¹²% and air cooling optimizer converged in under 1,000 iterations
how: Newton-Raphson: 4-stage decomposition strategy to solve 6 coupled nonlinear equations (condenser pressure, heat rate, power output) — overcame oscillation and divergence that defeated standard root-finding approaches; gradient descent reduced 9 constraints to 2 via algebraic substitution before minimization

RK-4 4th-order integration (O(h⁴) accuracy) applied to three compressible flow phenomena — area change, friction, and heat transfer — in variable-area ducts; Forward Euler integration of coupled spring-damper ODEs with sinusoidal and Gaussian forcing over 300 s; PID validated with practical op-amp R/C networks
results: Turbine cold-end system converged to 10⁻¹²% tolerance; air cooling cost minimized to $8,186.99 in <1,000 iterations

RK-4 compressible flow: subsonic and supersonic Mach responses to area change, friction, and heat transfer quantified and contrasted — directly applicable to rocket nozzle design; 3-mass shock damper: full transient-to-steady-state energy cascade tracked under sinusoidal and Gaussian forcing over 300 s
